message.content:
Create the following 5 page components in C:\Users\User\stan-io\pages\. Each page must use CSS variables (var(--bg-base), var(--text-primary), var(--accent), etc.) and import from the project's existing UI components and demo data.

IMPORTANT CONTEXT:
- Project location: C:\Users\User\stan-io
- UI components available: Button (from ../components/ui/Button), Card (from ../components/ui/Card), Badge (from ../components/ui/Badge), StatsCard (from ../components/ui/StatsCard), DataTable (from ../components/ui/DataTable), Tabs (from ../components/ui/Tabs), EmptyState (from ../components/ui/EmptyState), Modal (from ../components/ui/Modal), Toggle (from ../components/ui/Toggle)
- Demo data from ../lib/demo-data: demoEmailFlows, demoEmailBroadcasts, demoDiscountCodes, demoAnalytics, demoProducts
- Types from ../types: EmailFlow, EmailBroadcast, DiscountCode, AnalyticsData
- Use Tailwind CSS with CSS custom properties
- Icons from lucide-react  
- Every component is a default export
- For charts, use recharts: import { AreaChart, Area, BarChart, Bar, PieChart, Pie, Cell, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, ResponsiveContainer, Legend } from 'recharts'

Create these files:

## 1. pages/EmailFlows.tsx
- Page title "Email Flows" + "Create Flow" button
- Explain section: "Automated email sequences triggered by customer actions"
- Flow list showing demoEmailFlows as Cards: name, trigger type badge (purchase/signup/abandoned/manual), subject line, enabled toggle, stats (sent count, open rate %)
- Each flow card has Edit/Delete buttons
- Trigger type badges: purchase = accent, signup = success, abandoned = warning, manual = default
- Visual flow indicator: trigger icon → email icon → stats

## 2. pages/Broadcasts.tsx
- Page title "Broadcasts" + "New Broadcast" button
- Explain: "One-time email campaigns to your subscriber list"
- Broadcast list from demoEmailBroadcasts: subject, status badge (draft/scheduled/sent), recipient count, sent date, open rate, click rate
- Status badges: sent = success, scheduled = warning, draft = default
- "New Broadcast" form concept area: subject input, body textarea, recipient count display, schedule option
- Stats overview for sent broadcasts: delivery rate, open rate, click rate as progress bars

## 3. pages/DiscountCodes.tsx
- Page title "Discount Codes" + "Create Code" button
- Codes table using demoDiscountCodes: Code (monospace font, copyable), Type badge (percentage/fixed), Value ($100 or 10%), Usage (38/50 with progress bar if limit exists), Expires date, Products (specific or "All"), Active toggle
- "Create Code" form area: code input (with "Generate Random" button), type radio (percentage/fixed), value input, usage limit toggle + input, expiry date picker, product selector
- Usage statistics per code

## 4. pages/AnalyticsOverview.tsx
- Page title "Analytics" + period selector (7d, 14d, 30d, 90d) as tabs
- StatsCards row: Page Views (8,432, +15.2%), Unique Visitors (5,621, +11.8%), Sales (42, +22.1%), Revenue ($3,847, +18.4%), Conversion Rate (0.75%)
- Revenue + Views area chart using demoAnalytics.dailyData (dual Y axis: revenue as area, views as line). Use recharts.
- Top Products table: product name, sales, revenue (from demoAnalytics.topProducts)
- Traffic Sources bar chart using demoAnalytics.trafficSources (horizontal bars)
- All charts use var(--accent) as primary color: '#6C5CE7' for light mode

## 5. pages/TrafficSources.tsx
- Page title "Traffic Sources"
- Pie chart of traffic distribution from demoAnalytics.trafficSources using recharts PieChart
- Detailed source table: Source name, Visits, Percentage bar, % value
- Color-coded sources: Instagram = pink, TikTok = teal, Direct = gray, YouTube = red, Twitter = blue, Other = slate
- UTM builder section: source, medium, campaign input fields + generated URL preview
- Link performance section showing each source with visit count and trend arrows

Create the pages/ directory if needed, then write each file.

content.text: I'll start by exploring the existing project structure to understand the codebase, then create all 5 page components.

content.text: There's a type issue with the DataTable generic constraint. Let me fix the columns in DiscountCodes.tsx to match the `Record<string, unknown>` expected by `DataTable`.

The issue is that the `render` callback has `row: DiscountCode` but DataTable expects `row: Record<string, unknown>`. I need to cast inside the render functions. Let me fix this by using `Record<string, unknown>` in the column definitions and casting inside.

content.text:
All 5 pages are created, type-check cleanly, and build successfully. Here is a summary of each file:

---

### Files Created

**1. `C:\Users\User\stan-io\pages\EmailFlows.tsx`** (6.9 KB)
- Page title "Email Flows" with "Create Flow" button
- Description: "Automated email sequences triggered by customer actions"
- Cards for each flow from `demoEmailFlows` showing: name, trigger type badge (purchase=accent, signup=success, abandoned=warning, manual=default), subject line, enabled toggle, sent count, open rate
- Visual flow indicator: Trigger icon -> Email icon -> Stats icon in a left sidebar panel
- Edit/Delete buttons per flow card
- Toggle to enable/disable each flow with local state management

**2. `C:\Users\User\stan-io\pages\Broadcasts.tsx`** (11.3 KB)
- Page title "Broadcasts" with "New Broadcast" button
- Description: "One-time email campaigns to your subscriber list"
- Campaign performance section with progress bars for delivery rate, open rate, click rate
- Broadcast list from `demoEmailBroadcasts` with subject, status badge (sent=success, scheduled=warning, draft=default), recipient count, sent date, open/click rates
- Compose form area: subject input with icon, body textarea, recipient count display, schedule toggle with datetime picker, Send/Draft buttons

**3. `C:\Users\User\stan-io\pages\DiscountCodes.tsx`** (15.7 KB)
- Page title "Discount Codes" with "Create Code" button
- Usage statistics cards per code showing code name, usage count, and discount value
- DataTable with columns: Code (monospace + copy button), Type badge (percentage=accent, fixed=success), Value, Usage (progress bar when limit exists), Expires date, Products, Active toggle
- Create Code form: code input with "Generate Random" button, type radio (percentage/fixed), value input with prefix, usage limit toggle + input, expiry date picker, product selector dropdown
- All DataTable column render functions properly cast types for compatibility

**4. `C:\Users\User\stan-io\pages\AnalyticsOverview.tsx`** (9.7 KB)
- Page title "Analytics" with period selector tabs (7d, 14d, 30d, 90d) using the Tabs component
- StatsCards row: Page Views (+15.2%), Unique Visitors (+11.8%), Sales (+22.1%), Revenue (+18.4%), Conversion Rate (0.75%)
- Revenue & Views dual-axis AreaChart using recharts with gradient fills, `#6C5CE7` accent for revenue and green for views
- Top Products DataTable: product name, sales, revenue
- Traffic Sources horizontal BarChart using recharts with accent color bars

**5. `C:\Users\User\stan-io\pages\TrafficSources.tsx`** (12.6 KB)
- Page title "Traffic Sources" with description
- Donut PieChart of traffic distribution from `demoAnalytics.trafficSources` with color-coded sources (Instagram=pink, TikTok=teal, Direct=gray, YouTube=red, Twitter=blue, Other=slate)
- Detailed source breakdown table: colored dot, source name, visit count, percentage bar, percentage value
- Link Performance section: grid of source cards with visit count and trend arrows (up/down/neutral)
- UTM Builder section: source, medium, campaign input fields with live-generated URL preview and copy button
